meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop watching tutorials and start shipping. Use this SaaS starter kit guide with Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your MVP faster and reach revenue.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop consuming and start shipping. A SaaS starter kit provides the architectural scaffolding—auth, payments, and database—needed to bypass boilerplate. By leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code, you automate the heavy lifting, allowing you to focus entirely on your unique value proposition and revenue generation.
content.intro:
The screen glows with another 'To-Do List' tutorial, but your bank account remains stagnant. You’ve spent weeks mastering syntax, yet your product remains a ghost in your local directory. This is the silent killer of startups: Tutorial Hell, the endless cycle of learning without shipping.

Today, we pivot. You aren't here to learn how to code; you are here to build a business. We are stripping away the fluff and using professional-grade automation to move from zero to a deployed SaaS MVP in hours, not months. It’s time to stop being a student and start being a founder.

steps.title: Define Your Revenue-Generating Core
steps.description: Identify the one feature your users will pay for. Avoid scope creep by focusing on a single, high-impact problem.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Help me define the MVP scope for a [Insert Niche] SaaS. List only the 3 essential features required to validate the market and accept payments.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my project scope and identify any non-essential features that could delay my launch.'

steps.title: Initialize the SaaS Scaffolding
steps.description: Don't write boilerplate. Deploy a hardened starter kit that handles user authentication and database schemas out of the box.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a project structure for a SaaS MVP using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe. Include authentication, user profile management, and a secure payment gateway integration.

steps.title: Automate Database Design
steps.description: Let AI map your database schema to your business logic to ensure data integrity and scalability from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for my SaaS. Include tables for Users, Subscriptions, and [Core Feature Data]. Ensure all foreign keys and constraints are optimized for Supabase.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Create the SQL migration files for the schema defined in the schema.prisma file.'

steps.title: Integrate Payments for Revenue
steps.description: Connect Stripe to your app so you can start testing conversion immediately. Revenue is the ultimate validator.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Stripe webhook handler in Next.js to update user subscription status in Supabase when a payment event succeeds.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement the Stripe checkout session flow in the pricing page component.'

steps.title: Rapid Deployment & Feedback Loop
steps.description: Deploy to production immediately. An imperfect live app is worth more than a perfect local folder.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a checklist for deploying a Next.js app to Vercel, including environment variable management and domain configuration.

pro_tips: Prioritize 'Stripe-first' development; if you can't charge for it, the feature isn't worth building.
Use Claude Code to write tests for your payment flows before writing the UI to ensure financial integrity.
Don't build your own auth; use Clerk or Supabase Auth to save 40+ hours of security engineering.
Optimize for 'Time-to-First-Dollar'—if a feature takes more than 48 hours, cut it or simplify it.
Treat your codebase like a disposable asset; focus on the business logic, not the framework version.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, share it with 10 potential users in your niche. Collect feedback, iterate, and track your conversion rate. Do not add a single feature until you have your first paying customer.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation and the mindset to stop waiting and start winning. The market doesn't care about your code; it cares about your solution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
